title: galanthus nivalis subsp. allenii
intro: Galanthus nivalis subsp. allenii, also known as the Allen's snowdrop, is a subspecies of the snowdrop plant, a low-growing, bulbous perennial native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Galanthus nivalis subsp. allenii include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used externally to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other ailments.
